name: 80,000 Hours
focus: Publishes career research and problem profiles, runs a podcast and one-on-one advising, and now directs most of its effort at moving people into AI risk-reduction work.
notes: Feed 200 (application/rss+xml).

name: Access Now
focus: Global digital rights organisation working on spyware accountability, internet shutdowns, biometric ID systems and human-rights limits in the EU AI Act.
notes: Feed verified 200. Runs the Digital Security Helpline and #KeepItOn coalition.

name: Ada Lovelace Institute
focus: UK-focused research on AI regulation and public attitudes — foundation model accountability, biometrics law, AI in health and public services, and algorithmic audit/assurance methods.
notes: Feed returns valid RSS 2.0 XML (200).

name: Advanced Research and Invention Agency
focus: Funds the £59M Safeguarded AI programme, building an open-source mathematical assurance toolkit for formally verified AI-generated artifacts (TA1) and applying AI-enabled formal methods to cybersecurity (TA2).
notes: UK government R&D agency established under the ARIA Act 2022, operational January 2023. Safeguarded AI Programme Director is now Nora Ammann; David 'davidad' Dalrymple moved to Technical Advisor in April 2026. No RSS feed.

name: AE Studio
focus: Consultancy whose self-funded alignment team publishes neglected-approaches work — self-other overlap fine-tuning to reduce deception, and attention-schema/BCI-inspired methods.
notes: Alignment research page at /alignment (200). No RSS. Alignment work is a division inside a for-profit dev shop, not a standalone lab.

name: Aether
focus: Independent LLM agent safety group focused on chain-of-thought monitorability and faithfulness as an AI control mechanism.
notes: Domain resolves 200 but renders client-side (no server-side text). Announced Aug 2024, funded and formally launched 2025. No feed.

name: African Hub on AI Safety, Peace and Security
focus: University of Cape Town-hosted hub building African regional research and training capacity on AI safety and its peace-and-security implications.
notes: Page 200 under the UCT AI Initiative. Hosted within a university site, so no independent feed.

name: Agencia Española de Supervisión de la Inteligencia Artificial
focus: Acts as Spain's national market-surveillance authority for the EU AI Act, supervising high-risk AI systems placed on the Spanish market.
notes: Created by Royal Decree 729/2023 (22 August 2023), headquartered in A Coruña. Site is Spanish-language with no RSS; /es/actualidad is the news listing.

name: Agenzia per l'Italia Digitale
focus: Designated under Italian Law 132/2025 as the AI notification authority, defining procedures for assessing, accrediting and monitoring AI conformity-assessment bodies.
notes: HONEST FINDING: Italy has NO AI safety institute and is NOT a member of the international network. AgID founded 2012; its AI mandate dates from Law 132/2025, in force 10 October 2025. No RSS.

name: Agenzia per la Cybersicurezza Nazionale
focus: Italy's AI market-surveillance authority under Law 132/2025, with inspection and sanctioning powers; publishes AI security guidelines and hosted the EU ADCO AI market-surveillance cooperation group.
notes: Founded 2021; AI mandate from Law 132/2025. A real RSS exists at acn.gov.it/portale/feedrss/-/journal/rss/20119/723192 but it is 'RSS Csirt Italia' carrying vulnerability advisories, not AI or corporate news — hence the listing page is used instead. Banca d'Italia, CONSOB and IVASS are sectoral AI market-surveillance authorities.

name: AI & Democracy Foundation
focus: Builds and promotes deliberative and collective-intelligence tooling and governance practices so democratic institutions can absorb advanced AI rather than be destabilised by it.
notes: Founded by Aviv Ovadya. Site also served at ai-democracy.org (SPA that returns 200 for any path, so path-level verification is unreliable); related project site democracybuild.org. Main site has no RSS; the verified feed is their Substack newsletter. Founding year not confirmed.

name: AI Accountability Lab
focus: Trinity College Dublin lab led by Abeba Birhane doing empirical audits of training datasets and models (bias, hate speech, degenerate content) and mapping accountability gaps in AI supply chains.
notes: Hugo site; declared <link rel=alternate> to ./index.xml, verified as text/xml feed with ~14 entries. Founding year (2024) from secondary sources, not a primary page.

name: AI Alignment Forum
focus: Invite-to-post discussion forum (run on the LessWrong codebase) where alignment researchers publish technical write-ups on interpretability, deceptive alignment, agent foundations and evaluations.
notes: Declared feed at /feed.xml verified (/rss also works). A forum, not an org with staff or a research agenda of its own; risk vectors reflect the dominant subject matter of posts.

name: AI Community Africa
focus: Pan-African AI practitioner community running events and training, with some responsible-AI and safety programming.
notes: Live WordPress site with working /feed. Broad AI community rather than a dedicated AI safety org - included for African coverage with that caveat.

name: AI Digest
focus: Builds interactive explainers and live demonstrations (agent village, time-horizon and self-awareness evals) that show non-experts what current AI systems can and cannot do.
notes: Live with 2026 content (2025 forecast results, agent village, time horizons). No blog path or RSS; homepage is the index.

name: AI Impacts
focus: Publishes reference research on AI timelines and takeoff, including the recurring large-scale survey of published ML researchers' forecasts.
notes: WordPress site, feed declared in <link rel=alternate> and returns 200.

name: AI Now Institute
focus: Policy research and advocacy on the concentration of economic and infrastructural power in the AI industry, plus AI's effects on labour, surveillance, and the energy grid.
notes: WordPress; /feed returns application/rss+xml with items. Not to be confused with 'AI Now' as a generic phrase.

name: AI Objectives Institute
focus: Nonprofit lab studying misaligned optimisation in large-scale systems (AI, markets, bureaucracies) and building tools for collective input into AI objectives (e.g. Talk to the City).
notes: RSS verified, 20 items. Founded by the late Peter Eckersley.

name: AI Policy Institute
focus: Conducts and publishes US public opinion polling on AI regulation to inform legislators, consistently reporting majority support for slowing frontier development.
notes: Feed declared in homepage HTML; /reports and /blog paths 404, so the WordPress feed is the only listing endpoint.

name: AI Safety Asia
focus: Non-profit convening policymakers and researchers across Asia, running capacity-building programmes and publishing region-specific AI safety analysis.
notes: Correct domain is aisafety.asia (aisafetyasia.org/.com do not resolve). Substack feed 200, title 'AI Safety Asia'. Listed as a civil society organisation in UNESCO's ethics-of-AI directory. Country: UAE-headquartered per UNESCO's Global AI Ethics civil-society directory entry; the org's own site says only 'global non-profit ... for Asia'.

name: AI Safety Camp
focus: Long-running volunteer-run programme pairing newcomers into small remote research teams for a multi-week AI safety project; several orgs (e.g. Cadenza Labs) originated in its cohorts.
notes: Google Sites; editions listed through AISC10 (2025). Distributed/no fixed country. Outputs posted to LessWrong. No feed.

name: AI Safety Events & Training newsletter
focus: Weekly listing of AI safety fellowships, courses, conferences and deadlines, published by the AISafety.com team.
notes: Feed 200. The single best index of open field-building program deadlines.

name: AI Safety Fund
focus: Grantmaking fund established by the Frontier Model Forum and philanthropic partners that funds independent third-party research and evaluations on frontier model safety and security.
notes: Distinct from 'AI Safety Fundamentals' (BlueDot Impact), which shares the AISF initialism. Homepage links to frontiermodelforum.org; site notes administering partner Meridian Institute is suspending operations. /newsroom/feed/ verified RSS.

name: AI Safety Fund
focus: Grants $10M+ from Anthropic, Google, Microsoft and OpenAI plus outside foundations to independent researchers doing third-party frontier-model evaluations and safety/security research; a second cohort of 11 grantees received over $5M.
notes: DISAMBIGUATION: AISF has no standalone domain and lives on the Frontier Model Forum site. The separate site aisafetyfund.org is an unrelated 'AI Safety Research Fund' — do not use it. Administered by Meridian Institute until Meridian wound down in June 2025; FMF has run it directly since.

name: AI Safety India
focus: Builds an Indian AI safety community through training cohorts and research programmes aimed at local developers.
notes: Live (aisafetyindia.org 200; aisafetyindia.in does not resolve). Minimal single-page site focused on cohort signup; no feed.

name: AI Safety Newsletter (CAIS)
focus: Weekly-to-biweekly Substack summarising AI safety research, incidents and policy developments for a general audience.
notes: Feed 200, title 'AI Safety Newsletter', most recent item July 2026.

name: AI Safety Quest
focus: Offers free one-to-one 'navigation calls' that point newcomers to the courses, communities and jobs that fit them.
notes: Live; homepage states 'Since 2023, we have helped over 400 people'. No feed.

name: AI Safety Support
focus: Provides links, a Slack community and networking resources for early-career and independent AI safety researchers.
notes: Site returns 200 but is a static Google Sites page ('Lots of Links') with no dated content or feed; the project appears largely dormant, with its function taken over by AISafety.com and AI Safety Quest. Marked unconfirmed as an actively operating org.

name: AI Safety Tokyo
focus: Runs weekly AI safety study sessions (benkyoukai) in Japanese and English in Shibuya and organises the Technical AI Safety Conference (TAIS).
notes: /about states 'founded in January 2023 and is headquartered in Shibuya'. Related conference sites tais2024.cc and tais2025.cc exist. No RSS.

name: AI Safety, Ethics and Society textbook
focus: Free online textbook (Dan Hendrycks / CAIS) used as the base text for introductory AI safety courses and reading groups.
notes: Live. Static book site, no feed.

name: AI Security Institute
focus: Runs pre-deployment and post-deployment evaluations of frontier models for chemical/biological uplift, cyber-offence, autonomy and safeguard robustness, and publishes the underlying evaluation tooling (Inspect) and research findings.
notes: Renamed from 'AI Safety Institute' to 'AI Security Institute' in February 2025; acronym AISI retained. Established November 2023 out of the Frontier AI Taskforce. Site is a Webflow build with no RSS feed; /work is the research listing.

name: AI Snake Oil
focus: Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor's newsletter critiquing AI evaluation practice, benchmark claims and predictive-AI deployments; now branded 'AI as Normal Technology'.
notes: Feed 200; site title now reads 'AI as Normal Technology'.

name: AI Verify Foundation
focus: Maintains the AI Verify testing toolkit and Project Moonshot LLM evaluation/red-teaming tooling, and co-publishes Singapore's governance frameworks for generative AI with IMDA.
notes: Launched June 2023 by IMDA. Distinct from the Digital Trust Centre at NTU, which was designated Singapore's AI Safety Institute in May 2024. WordPress /feed/ returns HTTP 500 (feed disabled).

name: AI21 Labs
focus: Enterprise model developer whose published work centres on grounding and attribution to reduce hallucination in retrieval-augmented deployments.
notes: Frontier-adjacent lab; no publicly named internal safety/alignment team. No RSS found.

name: AISafety.com
focus: Maintains directories of AI safety projects, communities, funding and events, including the AI Safety Field Map at aisafety.world.
notes: Both aisafety.com (200) and the aisafety.world field map (200) are live. /projects 200. No RSS on the directory itself; its newsletter is listed separately.

name: Alexander von Humboldt Institute for Internet and Society
focus: Berlin academic institute researching AI and democracy (including a dataset of AI projects supporting democratic processes), DSA/platform power, and AI in the workplace.
notes: Feed verified; content is predominantly German-language.

name: Algorithmic Justice League
focus: Documenting demographic bias and harm in deployed AI, especially facial recognition and biometric systems in airports, policing, and hiring; runs a public AI-harms reporting channel.
notes: No RSS at any probed path; research library page verified 200.

name: AlgorithmWatch
focus: European watchdog investigating deployed algorithmic systems — content moderation labour, chatbot influence on elections and government decisions, data centre energy disclosure, and EU AI Act rollback.
notes: Feed verified; recent items include 'Could AI Chatbots influence a Government's Decisions?', Russian surveillance software in Georgia, and 'The AI Omnibus: a rollback of AI safeguards'.

name: Aligned AI
focus: Oxford-based lab working on concept extrapolation / goal generalisation out of distribution; now largely productised as 'Canvas'.
notes: Homepage 200, copyright 2025. Founded by Stuart Armstrong and Rebecca Gorman. Increasingly product-led (waitlist for Canvas); research page still present. No RSS.

name: Alignment Newsletter
focus: Rohin Shah's weekly summaries of new alignment-relevant papers, 2018-2022; the archive remains the standard index of that period's literature.
notes: DEFUNCT. Archive page returns 200 and states verbatim: 'The Alignment Newsletter is on indefinite hiatus.' alignmentnewsletter.substack.com 404s. rohinshah.com/feed returns 200 but is his general site feed, not newsletter issues. Its successor role is largely filled by the ML Safety Newsletter and the AI Safety Newsletter.

name: Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group
focus: Prague-based group (Charles University) studying alignment as a problem of hierarchical agency and multi-agent/ecosystem dynamics rather than single-model alignment.
notes: Included despite university affiliation because it operates as a self-contained research group with independent output. Posts index 200; no feed.

name: Alignment Research Center
focus: Theory-first alignment lab founded by Paul Christiano, working on eliciting latent knowledge, heuristic arguments / formal explanations of neural network behaviour, and low-probability estimation.
notes: This is alignment.org, NOT ARC Prize (arcprize.org) and not the ARC-AGI benchmark. Its former evaluations team spun out as METR in 2023. Declared feed at /blog/rss/; /blog/feed verified with ~15 items. Current leadership left deliberately unstated: alignment.org/team still names Paul Christiano as executive director, which appears stale given his move to the US AI Safety Institute, and no successor is named on the page.

name: Alignment Research Engineer Accelerator
focus: Intensive in-person bootcamp teaching the engineering skills for alignment work (transformers from scratch, mech interp, RL, evals); curriculum is public and widely reused.
notes: Training programme, not a publisher. Curriculum page 200; no feed.

name: Alliance for Securing Democracy
focus: German Marshall Fund programme tracking authoritarian interference in democracies, including state-linked information manipulation, AI-enabled influence tooling and the Authoritarian Tech Monitor.
notes: gmfus.org URL redirects to securingdemocracy.org; feed verified 200.

name: Amazon Web Services Responsible AI
focus: Internal AWS function producing AI Service Cards, Bedrock Guardrails content filtering and Automated Reasoning checks for hallucination detection in deployed model applications.
notes: Internal function of Amazon/AWS. Amazon's frontier models (Nova) ship with a separate responsible-AI documentation set.

name: American Civil Liberties Union
focus: Litigates and campaigns against government use of face recognition, algorithmic risk assessment in criminal justice and immigration, and AI-driven benefits determinations.
notes: Feed verified 200 application/rss+xml.

name: Amnesty Tech (Amnesty International)
focus: Amnesty's technology programme investigates spyware targeting of activists (Security Lab), facial recognition deployment, and algorithmic discrimination in welfare systems.
notes: Founded year is Amnesty International's; the Tech programme is more recent. Org-wide feed verified 200.

name: Andon Labs
focus: Real-world agentic evaluations — running frontier models as autonomous economic agents (Vending-Bench, the Anthropic 'Project Vend' shop, embodied robot evals) to find failure modes long-horizon benchmarks miss.
notes: Publications index 200; no RSS.

name: Anthropic Alignment Science
focus: Internal Anthropic team publishing empirical research on alignment faking, sabotage and sandbagging evaluations, model organisms of misalignment, and AI control protocols.
notes: Internal team of frontier lab Anthropic. The Alignment Science Blog is a separate property from anthropic.com/research and has a working Atom/RSS feed.

name: Anthropic Frontier Red Team
focus: Internal Anthropic team that measures Claude's dangerous capabilities in cyber operations, biosecurity and autonomous replication to inform Responsible Scaling Policy thresholds.
notes: Internal team of frontier lab Anthropic. Dedicated site red.anthropic.com launched 2025; feed.xml and rss.xml both return 200.

name: Anthropic Societal Impacts
focus: Internal Anthropic team producing the Anthropic Economic Index on labour-market effects of Claude usage, plus research on persuasion, political even-handedness and real-world value expression in model conversations.
notes: Internal team of frontier lab Anthropic. No standalone team URL — work appears under anthropic.com/research and anthropic.com/news. anthropic.com serves no RSS (rss.xml and index.xml both 404).

name: Apart Research
focus: Independent AI safety lab that runs global research hackathons/sprints and a fellowship, converting sprint outputs into peer-reviewed papers on evaluations, interpretability, deception and agent safety.
notes: Framer-hosted site, no RSS at any standard path; /research is the publications listing. /blog redirects to /news.

name: Apollo Research
focus: Builds behavioural evaluations for scheming and strategic deception in frontier models, and runs evals for frontier labs and AISIs.
notes: Homepage 200. /blog/feed/ returns HTML not XML; no working RSS found, so blog index used as page feed.

name: ARTICLE 19
focus: Global freedom-of-expression organisation working on AI content moderation, automated censorship, and AI provisions in internet governance and standards bodies (ITU, IETF).
notes: Homepage 403 to non-browser clients; /feed/ serves valid RSS with current items including 'UK: New prime minister must take a tough stance on AI and Big Tech'.

name: Article 36
focus: UK NGO producing legal and policy analysis on weapons that cause unacceptable harm, with a sustained workstream on meaningful human control over autonomous weapons and targeting systems.
notes: No RSS feed found (/feed 404); updates listing page verified 200. Co-founder of Stop Killer Robots.

name: Athena
focus: Runs a ten-week remote mentorship program in AI alignment research aimed specifically at women entering the field.
notes: Correct domain is researchathena.org (athenaresearch.org returns an empty page). Live with participants page and open applications.

name: Atlantic Council GeoTech Center
focus: Work on data and AI in geopolitics — trusted data flows, AI standards diplomacy, and technology statecraft among democracies.
notes: Feed is Atlantic Council-wide (declared in page HTML), not GeoTech-specific.

name: Australia's AI Safety Institute
focus: Monitors and analyses frontier-model capabilities including autonomous AI agents, tests new AI models and applications, and works with the Australian Signals Directorate and CSIRO, disseminating guidance through the National AI Centre.
notes: Announced 25 November 2025 with A$29.9m, operational from early 2026. Sits in the Department of Industry, Science and Resources; it is NOT a rename of the pre-existing National AI Centre. VERIFICATION CAVEAT: industry.gov.au is unreachable from this host via curl and WebFetch; page content confirmed live via Tavily extraction, but no direct HTTP 200 was obtained.

name: Axiom Math
focus: Builds AI mathematicians and formal-verification tooling (AXLE) for machine-checkable proofs — relevant to verified/guaranteed-safe AI approaches.
notes: Copyright 2025 on site. Capabilities-forward; safety relevance is via formal verification rather than an explicit alignment agenda. risk_vectors left empty: published output is mathematics/proof automation, not safety research.

name: Basis
focus: Nonprofit research org building probabilistic-programming and causal-inference machinery ('universal reasoning engine') and applying it to public-interest decision problems.
notes: Hugo RSS verified, 98 items. Safety relevance is indirect — foundations/agency research rather than frontier-model safety.

name: Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence
focus: Chinese state-backed AI research academy that authored the Beijing AI Principles and convenes the annual Beijing Academic Conference on AI safety and alignment alongside its model research.
notes: Primarily a research lab; included for its governance/principles output. No RSS. Chinese-language site is the primary; /en/ verified 200.

name: Berkeley Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence
focus: UC Berkeley center working on agentic AI foundations, LLM-agent safety and security (oversight, alignment, control, interpretability/monitoring), AI for cybersecurity and decentralization technology.
notes: rdi.berkeley.edu/feed returns a valid Atom document but it is an unconfigured Jekyll stub ('Your awesome title') with no items, so the research listing page is given instead. Risk vectors taken from the fetched Research page thrust list.

name: Berkman Klein Center for Internet & Society
focus: Harvard center studying platform governance, AI accountability and interpretability tooling, non-consensual intimate imagery reporting systems, child online safety and comparative AI regulation.
notes: Vectors mapped from fetched publication titles: 'Mapping the Burden: Toward Survivor-Centered Reporting Systems and Policies for Non-Consensual Intimate Images', 'Inside the Black Box' (interpretability dashboard), 'Vectors of AI Governance', 'Interventions for Online Harassment of Journalists'. /rss/rss.xml returns HTML, not a feed.

name: Big Brother Watch
focus: UK civil liberties group campaigning against live facial recognition in policing and retail, predictive policing, and expansion of state biometric databases.
notes: /feed and /rss return HTML rather than XML; using homepage news stream as the listing page.

name: BlueDot Impact
focus: Runs free facilitated online courses (the AI Safety Fundamentals alignment and governance curricula) that take professionals through AI risk material in structured cohorts.
notes: Their /about page states: started as Cambridge reading groups in 2021, founded BlueDot Impact 2022. aisafetyfundamentals.com now serves the same BlueDot site (200, identical title), so it is not listed separately.

name: Brookings Institution
focus: Publishes on defence AI, AI and nuclear deterrence, and AI governance through its Foreign Policy and Governance Studies programmes and the AI Equity Lab.
notes: Site declares /feed/ in head but that URL now returns HTML; AI topic listing page verified 200.

name: Cadenza Labs
focus: Small alignment group building robust lie detectors for LLMs — eliciting latent knowledge and detecting when a model states something it internally represents as false.
notes: Site live (28KB) but has no <title> tag. Grew out of an AI Safety Camp project.

name: Cambridge AI Safety Hub
focus: Student-and-researcher group at Cambridge running AI safety reading groups, technical bootcamps and research sprints.
notes: Live; no blog path or feed found.

name: Canadian Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute
focus: Funds and coordinates Canadian research on frontier AI risks through CIFAR and the National Research Council, focused on evaluation methods and misuse of advanced systems.
notes: Announced November 2024 by ISED with C$50M. Acronym CAISI collides with the renamed US body — disambiguate carefully. No RSS feed on the ISED page.

name: Canadian Centre for Child Protection
focus: Operates Cybertip.ca and the Project Arachnid crawler that detects and issues removal notices for child sexual abuse imagery, including synthetic material, across hosting providers.
notes: Child-protection charity. No RSS found; news releases listing page verified 200.

name: Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
focus: Its Technology and International Affairs programme maintains the AI Global Surveillance index and publishes on state adoption of AI surveillance and AI in geopolitics.
notes: /feed and /rss return HTML app shells; research listing page verified 200.

name: Cavendish Labs
focus: 501(c)(3) research community in Cavendish, Vermont working primarily on AI safety and pandemic prevention, with a research fellowship programme.
notes: Very small static site (2KB). Blog and papers pages 200; no feed. Fellowship listing on homepage.

name: Center for a New American Security
focus: US defence think tank whose Technology and National Security programme publishes on military AI adoption, autonomy in weapons, and AI's effect on nuclear stability and escalation.
notes: /feed and /rss both 404; publications listing page verified 200.

name: Center for AI and Digital Policy
focus: Assesses national AI policies against democratic-values criteria (the annual AI and Democratic Values Index), files regulatory complaints and comments, and runs an AI Policy Clinic training programme.
notes: The logo wall labels it 'Centre for AI and Digital Policy'; the organisation's own site uses 'Center'. Distinct from CAIS (safe.ai). Led by Marc Rotenberg and Merve Hickok. No RSS found; /reports/ is the publications listing.

name: Center for AI Policy
focus: Washington DC advocacy shop drafting model US federal legislation and briefing Congress on catastrophic risks from frontier AI systems (bio/chem uplift, cyber, autonomous agents).
notes: aipolicy.us redirects to centeraipolicy.org. Webflow site, no RSS; /work is the output listing. Site's last publish timestamp in HTML is Aug 2025, so publication cadence may have slowed.

name: Center for AI Risk Management & Alignment
focus: Small nonprofit translating frontier-AI risk into usable risk-management practice — profiles and guidance for applying the NIST AI Risk Management Framework to general-purpose and foundation models.
notes: Homepage <title> confirms 'Center for AI Risk Management & Alignment'. GoDaddy-hosted site with no RSS; /research-highlights is the output listing. Founding year could not be confirmed (a search-engine answer claiming 2017 looked fabricated and is not used here). Not to be confused with the CARMA marketing-analytics association or academic CARMA programmes.

name: Center for AI Safety
focus: Independent nonprofit doing technical safety research (WMDP unlearning benchmark, representation engineering, Humanity's Last Exam) alongside field-building and the AI Safety Newsletter.
notes: Homepage 200; on-domain feeds absent, but the CAIS-run AI Safety Newsletter feed at newsletter.safe.ai/feed verified as XML. Research index at /research. Has a separate policy arm (CAIS Action Fund) not covered here.

name: Center for Artificial Intelligence, University of Sao Paulo
focus: USP centre (FAPESP/IBM-funded) working on Portuguese-language NLP, AI in health and agriculture, and contributing to Brazil's OBIA AI observatory.
notes: Homepage verified 200 but renders almost entirely via JavaScript, so no publication titles could be extracted; risk_vectors left empty. Participation in OBIA corroborated by the Brazilian AI Observatory description.

name: Center for Democracy & Technology
focus: US and Brussels advocacy on AI civil rights — algorithmic discrimination in hiring and benefits, AI in education and elections, and EU AI Act medical/biometric scope.
notes: Homepage returns 403 to non-browser clients (Cloudflare) but /feed/ serves valid RSS with current items (civil rights letter to the House Democratic AI Commission, AI Act medical device letter).

name: Center for Human-Compatible Artificial Intelligence
focus: UC Berkeley research centre (Stuart Russell) developing the assistance-game / uncertain-objective framing of alignment, plus reward learning, value misspecification, and multi-agent alignment research.
notes: /feed verified RSS with items.

name: Center for Humane Technology
focus: Advocacy nonprofit (Tristan Harris, Aza Raskin) campaigning on attention-capture and persuasive design in social media and now on companion chatbots, youth harms, and AI product liability/litigation.
notes: Next.js site with no on-domain RSS; their Substack feed is verified RSS. Also produce the 'Your Undivided Attention' podcast and The AI Dilemma / Social Dilemma media.

name: Center for Information Technology Policy
focus: Princeton center bridging computer science and public policy on algorithmic accountability, AI evaluation and measurement, platform regulation and the societal effects of automated decision systems.
notes: Site sits behind a Cloudflare interstitial: curl and WebFetch both return 403 'Just a moment...'. Existence and activity corroborated via search results only, so no publication titles were fetched and risk_vectors is deliberately left empty.

name: Center for Safe and Trustworthy AI, Shanghai AI Laboratory (SafeWork / AI45)
focus: Shanghai AI Lab's safety centre publishing the Frontier AI Risk Management Framework, SafeWork safety-reasoning training systems, embodied-AI safety simulation and multi-model frontier risk evaluations.
notes: Research listing verified 200 with posts 'SafeWork-F1: A Frontier AI Risk Management Framework', 'SafeWork-T1', 'SafeVerse Open Source'. The framework report (arXiv 2507.16534, with Concordia AI) evaluated 20+ models across cyber offense, bio/chem, persuasion, autonomous R&D, strategic deception, self-replication and collusion. State-backed lab rather than a degree-granting university.

name: Center for Security and Emerging Technology
focus: Georgetown research center producing data-driven analysis of military AI adoption, AI test and evaluation for defence, chip supply chains, and AI-cyber capability.
notes: Feed verified 200.

name: Center for Strategic and International Studies
focus: Foreign-policy think tank with defence-AI, AI export control and semiconductor supply-chain analysis via its Wadhwani AI Center and Strategic Technologies Program.
notes: rss.xml verified 200 application/rss+xml.

name: Center on Long-Term Risk

notes: Usually abbreviated CLR, not COLTR; COLTR is the logo wall's label (and is easy to confuse with CLTR, the Centre for Long-Term Resilience, listed separately). Founded 2013 as the Foundational Research Institute, renamed 2019. Declared WordPress feed verified with ~148 items.

name: Centre for AI and Digital Ethics
focus: University of Melbourne cross-faculty centre (law, engineering, arts, science) on regulation and ethics of AI and digital technologies, with associated postgraduate teaching.
notes: Site sits behind a Cloudflare interstitial (403 to curl); existence, faculty composition and founding year (2020) corroborated via University of Melbourne newsroom and dataservices listings. No publication titles fetched, so risk_vectors left empty.

name: Centre for European Policy Studies
focus: Brussels think tank producing task-force reports on EU AI Act design, standardisation and liability, and on AI's effect on EU competitiveness and labour markets.
notes: No RSS found; publications listing verified 200.

name: Centre for Future Generations
focus: European think tank on emerging-technology risk for policymakers, covering frontier AI governance, cognitive integrity/manipulation, biotech, and climate interventions.
notes: icfg.eu redirects to cfg.eu (International Centre for Future Generations shortened its name). Feed verified; recent items include 'Cognitive integrity is the next frontier in AI governance' and a submission to the UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance.

name: Centre for International Governance Innovation
focus: Canadian think tank on international technology governance — AI and trade rules, data governance regimes, and AI's effect on democratic institutions and information ecosystems.
notes: Homepage 200; /publications and AI topic subpages return 403 to non-browser clients (Cloudflare). Publications index confirmed to exist via search. No RSS discoverable.

name: Centre for Responsible AI, IIT Madras

Head: Balaraman Ravindran, within the Wadhwani School of Data Science and AI.

name: Centre for the Governance of AI

notes: Feed declared in homepage HTML at /post/rss.xml and verified. Founded 2018 within the Future of Humanity Institute; became an independent organisation in 2021.

name: Centre for the Study of Existential Risk

name: CERT Coordination Center (SEI)

name: Singapore AI Safety Institute
focus: Runs multilingual and multicultural AI evaluation and agentic-AI testing via the Moonshot toolkit, studies emotional dependency and cognitive offloading in AI use, and ran the Singapore AI Safety Red-Teaming Challenge.
notes: Operated by the Digital Trust Centre (DTC) at Nanyang Technological University in partnership with IMDA; designated Singapore's AI Safety Institute on 22 May 2024. DTC itself launched 1 November 2022 with a S$50m IMDA/NRF grant. Distinct from the AI Verify Foundation entry. TRAPS: dtc.ntu.edu.sg does not exist; sgaisi.sg/feed/ returns 200 but serves a 404 HTML page.

name: Softmax
focus: Independent lab building Cogames, an open multi-agent benchmark for social intelligence, on the thesis that multi-agent social capability is needed for scalable alignment.
notes: Homepage 200 with open-source benchmark and PufferLib-based trainer. No RSS.

name: SpaceXAI Safety
focus: Internal safety function of the Grok developer, publishing model cards, a Frontier AI Framework and pre-deployment safety evaluations.
notes: Risk vectors taken from the text of their own Frontier AI Framework PDF, which covers CBRN weapons, cyberattack uplift, loss of control and jailbreak resistance. IMPORTANT RENAME: xAI was acquired by SpaceX (Feb 2026) and rebranded as SpaceXAI on 6 July 2026; the x.ai domain and Grok product name are retained. Page verified via Tavily extract (Cloudflare 403s curl). Frontier AI Framework PDF dated 2025-12-31 at data.x.ai.

name: Spawning AI
focus: Builds consent infrastructure for training data — Have I Been Trained, the Do Not Train registry, and the ai.txt/Spawning API used by dataset builders to honour opt-outs.
notes: /feed and /rss return the HTML app shell; blog listing page verified 200.

name: Stanford Center for AI Safety
focus: Formal verification, safe learning and control, and transparency/explainability methods for autonomous and intelligent systems, plus AI governance and human-oversight research.
notes: Founding date 2020 read from schema.org JSON-LD embedded in the homepage. No feed found on any standard path; /research/ returns Stanford's 404 page, so no usable publications listing was confirmed. Distinct from the standalone nonprofit Center for AI Safety (safe.ai).

name: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence
focus: Interdisciplinary research and policy analysis on AI's societal effects, including the annual AI Index measurement report and policy briefs for US and international regulators.
notes: No RSS/Atom on any standard path. Publications page is a JS-driven filter UI, so individual paper titles could not be extracted by curl; risk vectors kept minimal rather than inferred. Runs aiindex.stanford.edu (verified 200).

name: Stanford Tech Impact & Policy Center
focus: Stanford FSI center on social technology and AI; publishes the Journal of Online Trust and Safety and runs the annual Trust and Safety Conference, continuing the trust-and-safety research line formerly housed in the Stanford Internet Observatory.
notes: The Stanford Internet Observatory URL (cyber.fsi.stanford.edu/io) now redirects here. SIO was substantially wound down/restructured after 2024; its CSAM and influence-operations research lines do not continue in the same form. Founding year of the TIP Center is approximate.

name: Statewatch
focus: Monitors European state and EU-level policing, borders and justice policy, including AI-driven border surveillance, Europol data systems and biometric interoperability.
notes: /rss verified 200 RSS; /feed returns an HTML error page.

name: SteadRise (formerly Impact Academy)
focus: Recruits and accelerates mid-career operators, researchers and policymakers into AI safety and governance roles, with programmes originally targeting India and the Global South.
notes: Impact Academy rebranded to SteadRise in 2026; impactacademy.org now serves the SteadRise site. No feed found. HQ country not stated on the site; earlier Impact Academy programming was India-focused and current leadership is listed as US-based, so country left as International rather than guessed.

name: Stockholm International Peace Research Institute
focus: Research programme on emerging military technologies covering AI in nuclear command and control, autonomy in weapon systems, and export controls on military AI.
notes: /rss and /rss.xml do not return XML; publications listing page verified 200.

name: Stop Killer Robots
focus: Coalition of ~250 NGOs campaigning for a legally binding international treaty prohibiting autonomous weapons systems that select and engage targets without meaningful human control.
notes: Homepage and RSS feed both return 200. /feed returns empty; /rss is the working feed.

name: StopNCII.org
focus: Victim-support service run by SWGfL that generates on-device hashes of intimate images so participating platforms can block their non-consensual sharing, including synthetic and AI-altered images.
notes: Victim-support tool rather than a publishing organisation; no news or blog section found (all probed paths 404). Operated by SWGfL (swgfl.org.uk).

name: Supervised Program for Alignment Research
focus: Runs biannual part-time remote mentorship cohorts pairing early-career people with AI safety and AI policy researchers on concrete research projects; now operated by Kairos.
notes: Risk vectors left empty on purpose: SPAR publishes nothing under its own name — output is mentee projects spread across the whole of AI safety and AI policy, so any mapping would be plausibility rather than evidence. Homepage title 'SPAR - Research Program for AI Risks'. No RSS feed; /projects lists each cohort's project slate (redirects to current cohort, e.g. /projects/sp26/). Founding year (~2022, originating from the Berkeley AI Safety Initiative) not confirmed from a primary source.

name: Surveillance Technology Oversight Project
focus: New York-based litigation and advocacy group challenging NYPD surveillance tools, facial recognition in venues and housing, and geolocation tracking.
notes: Squarespace site; no RSS found, blog listing page verified 200.

name: Survival and Flourishing Fund
focus: Allocates donor funds (chiefly Jaan Tallinn's) to existential-risk organisations via the S-Process; SFF-2025 recommended $1.607M to MIRI, $1.061M to the Center for AI Safety, $919k to FAR AI and $1.5M to SecureDNA.
notes: ~$152M organised since 2019; SFF-2026 round totalled $34.33M recommended. No RSS; /recommendations is the grants listing.

name: Takshashila Institution
focus: Independent Indian public-policy school and think tank with a High-Tech Geopolitics programme covering AI regulation, semiconductors, and technology sovereignty.
notes: Homepage 200 but every probed publications/research/blog path 404s and no RSS resolves; only the homepage is verifiable as a listing.

name: Talos Network
focus: Places fellows in European AI governance policy roles and runs an EU AI Governance Fundamentals course and policy leaders programme.
notes: Live; /perspectives is the writing page. No RSS.

name: Tarbell Center for AI Journalism
focus: Funds and trains journalists to cover AI, placing fellows at major newsrooms and providing grants for AI accountability reporting.
notes: Live at tarbellfellowship.org (www subdomain does not resolve). Single-page site; no feed.

name: Tech Policy Press
focus: Non-profit publication and convener for tech and democracy policy debate; a primary venue where AI governance researchers and advocates publish analysis.
notes: Media/publishing organisation rather than a research institute — included as ecosystem infrastructure. Feed verified as valid RSS; a podcast feed also exists at feeds.captivate.fm/techpolicypress/.

name: The Alan Turing Institute
focus: UK national institute for data science and AI, with programmes on AI safety and security (including work with the UK AI Security Institute), public policy and defence applications.
notes: Homepage verified 200 but /research/publications and /news/rss.xml both return Cloudflare 403 to curl, so no publication titles were fetched and risk_vectors is left empty.

name: The Alignment Project (UK AISI)
focus: UK AI Security Institute programme that funds external alignment research grants against a published research agenda; has announced funding for ~60 projects.
notes: Live at alignmentproject.aisi.gov.uk (alignmentproject.uk does not resolve). Parent AISI site is www.aisi.gov.uk (200); www.aisi.ac.uk does not resolve.

name: The Authors Guild
focus: US writers' organisation litigating against AI developers over unlicensed use of books in training data and publishing model contract clauses on AI rights for authors.
notes: Feed verified 200.

name: The Centre for Long-Term Resilience
focus: UK policy institute advising government on extreme-risk management, with workstreams on frontier AI regulation and incident reporting, biosecurity, and cross-government risk-ownership reform.
notes: Site sits behind a Cloudflare bot challenge (403/202 to curl and to WebFetch), so page content could not be fetched directly; existence and the /reports listing URL confirmed via search index (title 'Reports Archive - CLTR'). Site is WordPress so an RSS feed likely exists at /feed but could not be verified, hence feed_kind=page.

name: The Future Society
focus: Practical AI governance implementation work with EU institutions, OECD, GPAI and the UN, focused on the EU AI Act's general-purpose AI rules and international coordination.
notes: Feed URL declared in homepage HTML and returns 200, but SiteGround bot protection serves a captcha interstitial to non-browser clients; /publications/ is the fallback listing page.

name: The Laboratory for Human-Centered AI (Oxford HAI Lab)
focus: Oxford philosophy-based lab (director Philipp Koralus) studying human reasoning and machine reasoning together — e.g. the PyETR reasoning framework, 'humans in, humans out' work, and AI agency/autonomy questions.
notes: NOT Stanford HAI — the logo wall links to hailab.ox.ac.uk. Funded in part by Cosmos Institute (hosts the 'Cosmos HAI Lab Lecture'). WordPress but /feed/ returns HTML, not RSS; /project/ is the research listing.

name: Thorn
focus: Builds detection and classification tooling for child sexual abuse material and leads the Safety by Design initiative committing generative AI developers to prevent models producing abuse imagery.
notes: Child-protection technology nonprofit. Blog feed verified 200.

name: Tilde Research
focus: Mechanistic interpretability lab also working on new architectures and pretraining science; publishes feature/circuit-level analyses of production models.
notes: www.tilderesearch.com redirects to apex domain. No RSS found.

name: Timaeus
focus: Applies singular learning theory and developmental interpretability to measure phase transitions and structure formation during neural network training.
notes: Homepage banner as of fetch: 'Timaeus is merging into Resolution' (link to resolution.org/launch). Still operating and hiring at time of check, but the org is being absorbed — see the Resolution entry.

name: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change
focus: Pro-adoption policy advice to governments on AI in public services, state capacity, and national AI infrastructure; a notable counterweight voice to restriction-first AI regulation.
notes: No RSS found; /insights listing verified 200.

name: Trail of Bits
focus: Security research firm auditing ML frameworks and model serialization formats, publishing on model file exploitation, ML supply-chain attacks and LLM agent sandbox escapes.
notes: Blog feed verified 200 (application/xml).

name: Trajan House
focus: Oxford coworking building operated by the Centre for Effective Altruism that hosts longtermist and AI safety organisations and events.
notes: Correct domain is trajan.house (200); trajanhouse.org does not resolve. No feed.

name: Transformer
focus: Original reporting and analysis outlet (Shakeel Hashim) covering AI labs, safety policy and industry politics, including a campaign finance tracker.
notes: Feed 200, latest item 17 July 2026. Self-described as 'a publication about the power and politics of transformative AI'.

name: Transluce
focus: Builds open tooling for understanding model behaviour at scale — Docent for automated analysis of agent transcripts, plus neuron/behaviour description and elicitation methods.
notes: Homepage 200; recent items dated Jul 2026 ('Toward a Public Science of Model Behavior', 'Predictive Concept Decoders'). No RSS found.

name: TRUE AI Lab (Trustworthy and Responsible AI Lab)
focus: Oregon State University lab studying AI security and privacy: reward poisoning, backdoors, membership inference, indirect prompt injection against web agents and LLM detoxification.
notes: Also reachable at https://secure-ai.systems/ which serves the same Atom feed. Vectors from fetched publication titles including 'When Can You Poison Rewards?', 'AgentBreaker: ... Indirect Prompt Injection Risks in Modern Web Agents', 'Privacy Backdoors'. Directed by Sanghyun Hong.

name: Tsinghua University Institute for AI International Governance
focus: Chinese academic institute on international AI governance; produces AI governance blue books and Track II dialogue with Western institutions on frontier AI risk and military AI.
notes: English site verified 200; deeper research subpaths 404, so the English landing page is the listing. No feed.

name: TU Delft Department of Values, Technology and Innovation
focus: Value-sensitive design and ethics of technology group at TU Delft working on responsible AI design, moral responsibility for autonomous systems and AI in public administration.
notes: Page verified 200. A separately branded 'Delft AI Safety Institute' URL returns 404, so it is not listed. No feed.

name: Turkopticon
focus: Worker-run organisation and review tool for Amazon Mechanical Turk crowdworkers, letting workers rate requesters and collectively contest unfair rejections and wage theft.
notes: /feed returns HTML; blog listing page verified 200. Worker-governed nonprofit since 2021.

name: Understanding AI
focus: Timothy B. Lee's reported newsletter explaining AI systems and their real-world deployment, often fact-checking capability claims.
notes: Feed 200.

name: United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research
focus: UN research institute whose Security and Technology Programme publishes on autonomous weapons, AI in nuclear risk, and military AI governance for state delegations.
notes: Feed verified 200 (text/xml).

name: United Nations — AI Advisory Body / Independent International Scientific Panel on AI
focus: UN Secretary-General's multistakeholder body whose 'Governing AI for Humanity' reports underpin the Global Dialogue on AI Governance and the new independent scientific panel.
notes: Page verified 200; no feed. Successor structures (Global Dialogue, Scientific Panel) were established by GA resolution in 2025.

name: Vector Institute for Artificial Intelligence
focus: Toronto institute doing machine learning research with a responsible-AI programme covering bias detection and rewriting, model evaluation and trustworthy deployment tooling.
notes: Feed verified XML; items include 'Vector Institute releases UnBias-Plus, a free, open-source AI tool to detect and rewrite bias in text' and ICML 2026 responsible-AI work.

name: Verification Research, Training and Information Centre
focus: Independent research on verification and compliance mechanisms for arms control and disarmament treaties, including how emerging technologies affect verification of weapons agreements.
notes: Feed verified 200. AI-specific output is limited; core relevance is verification methodology for autonomous-weapons and arms-control regimes.

name: Virtue AI
focus: Enterprise AI security platform — automated red-teaming (VirtueRed), runtime guardrails (VirtueGuard) and agentic-system security/compliance tooling.
notes: Spun out of academic work on trustworthy ML (DecodingTrust lineage). Commercial; blog 200, no RSS.

name: WASP-HS (Wallenberg AI, Autonomous Systems and Software Program - Humanities and Society)
focus: Swedish national research programme funding humanities and social science work on AI, including global AI governance institutions, AI in policing, and AI and war.
notes: Feed verified. Items: 'WASP-HS Study Visit to the Swedish Police Authority', 'The Global Governance of Artificial Intelligence: Actor Preferences, Bargaining Dynamics, and Institutional Design', 'Launching WASP-HS Research Network on AI and War'.

name: Weizenbaum Institute for the Networked Society
focus: German national research network on digitalisation and society covering AI in the public sphere, democracy and digital work.
notes: Homepage verified 200. No RSS/Atom found; verified 200 listing page given instead; risk_vectors left empty pending publication-level verification.

name: WeProtect Global Alliance
focus: Multi-stakeholder alliance of governments, companies and civil society coordinating national responses to online child sexual exploitation and publishing the Global Threat Assessment, which tracks generative-AI abuse material.
notes: Child-protection coordination body formed by merger of the WePROTECT initiative and the Global Alliance. Feed verified 200.

name: WITNESS
focus: Human rights video organisation whose 'Prepare, Don't Panic' programme works on deepfake preparedness, detection access for frontline journalists in the global majority, and provenance standards that do not endanger at-risk documenters.
notes: Feed verified 200. Runs a deepfake rapid-response detection escalation pathway.

name: Z.ai (Zhipu AI)
focus: Chinese frontier lab behind the GLM model family; safety-relevant output is confined to technical reports and evaluation sections accompanying open-weight releases.
notes: RENAME: Zhipu AI (Beijing Zhipu Huazhang) now brands internationally as Z.ai. z.ai/blog returns 404 and there is no discoverable RSS; the zai-org GitHub organisation is the working publication listing.
